title: Implement secure token caching and auto-refresh logic
description: Once tokens are obtained from the OAuth callback, save them securely to a local config directory (e.g., ~/.config/drivectl/token.json). Implement logic that checks token expiration upon initialization; if the token is nearing expiration, use the refresh token to silently obtain a new access token before making API calls.
close_reason: Updated internal/drive/auth.go to cache secrets and tokens in ~/.config/drivectl

title: Build 'auth login' command and local OAuth callback server
description: Create a new command 'drivectl auth login' that spins up a temporary local HTTP server (e.g., localhost:8080) to handle the Google OAuth2 callback. It should construct the authorization URL and automatically open the user's default browser to consent to the requested scopes.
close_reason: Implemented cmd/auth.go with login command

title: Epic: Robust Authentication System
description: Implement a more user-friendly and robust authentication system. This includes an interactive 'auth login' command that opens a browser for OAuth2 flow, secure local token caching (with refresh tokens), and automatic token refresh logic to eliminate the need for passing the secrets file on every invocation.
close_reason: All underlying tasks (lfx, uls, 8fd) are completed.

title: Create RELEASING.md documentation
description: Create docs/RELEASING.md to outline the step-by-step instructions for human maintainers on how to push tags and trigger a new release, modeled after the a2acli documentation.
close_reason: Created docs/RELEASING.md based on a2acli's release process.

title: Configure GoReleaser and GitHub Actions

close_reason: Added .goreleaser.yaml and .github/workflows/release.yaml

title: Epic: Automate Release Process
description: Incorporate the releasing process with tagging, version injection, and cross-platform artifact building similar to the a2acli sister project. This will allow end-users to easily download versioned binaries without needing a full Go toolchain.
close_reason: Implemented goreleaser and github actions workflow, and documented the process in RELEASING.md

title: Feature: Support Service Account Authentication
description:
Implement support for Google Cloud Service Account (JSON key) authentication as an alternative to the user OAuth2 flow. This addresses GitHub Issue #8.

Changes required:
1. Update 'internal/drive/auth.go' to expose a 'NewServiceAccountClient' function that uses 'google.JWTConfigFromJSON'.
2. Add a global flag '--service-account' (or check 'GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS') to 'cmd/root.go'.
3. Update the 'PersistentPreRunE' auth router to check for this flag and branch into the service account initialization path instead of the standard OAuth flow.
4. Update docs and README to explain the usage.

title: Implement proactive error guidance
description: Audit common failure points (e.g., unauthenticated, missing flags, invalid APIs) and wrap errors with actionable 'Hint:' lines guiding the user on how to resolve the issue.
close_reason: Implemented ui.ErrorWithHint for proactive error guidance

title: Refactor MCP tools to use Discovery JSON schemas
description: Update the MCP server implementation. Instead of manually defining structs like DocsCreateArgs, dynamically generate the tool parameter schemas based on the parsed Discovery Documents. This will ensure the LLM receives accurate and comprehensive type information.
close_reason: Refactored MCP server to dynamically generate and register tool schemas from Discovery Documents

title: Expose generic 'call' subcommand with local schema validation
description: Create a new 'drivectl call <service.resource.method>' subcommand. It should accept a JSON payload, validate it against the cached Discovery Document schema before sending the request, and return the raw JSON response.
close_reason: Created cmd/call.go that leverages Discovery Docs to dynamically validate payload and construct API requests

title: Implement a Discovery Document fetcher and cacher
description: Build a utility to fetch JSON schema definitions from Google's Discovery API (e.g., https://www.googleapis.com/discovery/v1/apis/drive/v3/rest). Parse these schemas into Go structs and cache them locally (with a TTL, e.g., 24 hours) to avoid repetitive network calls.
close_reason: Implemented internal/discovery/discovery.go for fetching and caching discovery docs

title: Epic: API Completeness via Discovery Documents
description: Enhance the CLI to support dynamic API interactions by fetching, caching, and parsing Google API Discovery Documents. This will allow the CLI to validate JSON payloads locally, expose a generic 'call' subcommand for arbitrary endpoints, and automatically generate comprehensive schemas for the MCP server tools.
close_reason: All underlying tasks for API completeness (Discovery fetcher, call command, dynamic MCP) are complete.

title: Implement versioning and lifecycle tracking

close_reason: Added Version tracking to root command

title: Implement visual information design and semantic colors
description: Introduce a standard semantic color palette (Pass, Warn, Fail, Muted, Accent) utilizing a library like fatih/color. Update standard CLI text output to utilize these tokens to improve the data-ink ratio and scannability.
close_reason: Created internal/ui/theme.go with Ayu semantic color palette

title: Implement Google Doc to Markdown converter
description: Build functionality to export a Google Doc back into Markdown format by parsing the Google Docs AST (paragraphs, formatting, lists, headings) into standard Markdown.
close_reason: Updated renderBodyAsText to parse Doc AST and export Markdown instead of plain text

title: Build framework for composable CLI recipes
description: Add a mechanism (e.g., 'drivectl run <recipe>') to execute predefined sequences of API calls (e.g., downloading a sheet, transforming it, uploading to a backup folder) to simplify complex workflows.
close_reason: Implemented cmd/run.go with JSON recipe parsing and sequential command execution

title: Epic: Advanced Workflows, Recipes, and Skills
description: Elevate drivectl from simple commands to advanced workflows. Add bidirectional Markdown conversion, support for composable CLI 'recipes' (chained API calls), and bundle Agent Skills (e.g., SKILL.md files) to give LLMs contextual awareness of how to use the tool for complex tasks.
close_reason: All underlying Advanced Workflow tasks (json output, markdown export, recipes) are complete
